ref: The disciples left and did what Jesus had told them to do.
text: Na i susu­fɔm n akɔ ayo kabo i seeri bu ni.
translated: The child's mother did not give him any food before he left.

ref: Don't store up treasures on earth! Moths and rust can destroy them, and thieves can break in and steal them.
text: Na Yesu akaaki ase ka, “Má am tiɛn ajɛkɛ nɔɔ se durunya n nu fɛn, bo watarawam muka i na i kpɔrɔ, na i tɔ ŋgambulaari, na awiefɔm gusu bá wura wɔ i.
translated: When Jesus had finished saying these things, “Do not let your hearts be troubled. You believe in God; believe also in me. My Father’s house has many rooms; if that were not so, would I have told you that I am going there to prepare a place for you?

ref: A good tree produces good fruit, and a bad tree produces bad fruit.
text: Ama, i ti sɔ ɔ lɛɛ, ka baka kpa kɛrɛ su suu­wa kpa a, na baka ŋgbɛɛn kɛrɛ su suu­wa ŋgbɛɛn­ŋgbɛɛn.
translated: Ama, if you don't want it, then don't buy the white one, but buy the black one.

ref: So the demons begged Jesus, “If you force us out, please send us into those pigs!”
text: na ajinim n asere Yesu ka, “Nzɛn a ŋa a fɔn ya fite minɛm n ŋu, ma ya kɔ fun afam n ŋu.”
translated: When Jesus saw the crowd, he went up the mountain, and after he had sat down, his disciples came to him.

ref: The people of all nations will be brought before him, and he will separate them, as shepherds separate their sheep from their goats.
text: Na durunya n nu minɛm kɛrɛ bá tiɛn bu nɔɔ m nyunu, na m kpáaki bu nu, busu nnyɔ, kabo bɔɛ­sasa­fɔ kpaaki yi nbɔɛm ni sirim nu barasu ni.
translated: If you don't have the strength to carry your load, and you ask someone to help you, they will come, but they will not carry it for you; they will only help you carry it.

translated: When Herod died, an angel of the Lord appeared to Joseph in a dream in Egypt and said, "Get up, take the child and his mother, and go to the land of Israel, for those who were trying to take the child's life are dead."

ref: Would any of you give your hungry child a stone, if the child asked for some bread?
text: Ambɛrɛ nu, ŋma ye i wa sere i diire, ye i mi kéte na i fá yabuɛ ma i wa n a? Ai, be yo má sɔ.
translated: Now, if you have a child and you don't take care of them, and you don't even give them food, what will happen? Oh, it's not good.

ref: The servants came and asked, “Sir, didn't you scatter good seed in your field? Where did these weeds come from?”

translated: When the old woman saw the child, she asked, 'My child, I want to send you to the farm to fetch firewood. Can you go with your friends to cut the firewood and bring it back home?'

translated: "When Peter saw this, he said to Jesus, 'Lord, what about this man?' Jesus answered, 'If I want him to remain alive until I return, what is that to you? You must follow me.' ref: When they got there, Jesus was told that Simon's mother-in-law was sick in bed with fever.

translated: When they had finished eating, Jesus said to Simon Peter, "Simon son of John, do you love me more than these?" "Yes, Lord," he said, "you know that I love you."

ref: Everyone who has something will be given more, and they will have more than enough. But everything will be taken from those who don't have anything.
text: Dama sɔnɔ kɛrɛ bo wɔ nya deke na i mi si i bita, bu má i buka su wɔ, na i nyá beberebe, ama, bo i si má i bita, haari ni nyini kaan n bo i la i ni, bu cín de i dɔ ɔ.
translated: If you see that your friend is in trouble and you don't help him, but you just stand there and watch, and then you see that he is in danger, and you don't help him, then you are not a good friend.

ref: While Jesus was still speaking, some people came from Jairus' home and said, “Your daughter has died! Why bother the teacher anymore?”
text: Saŋga n bo Yesu toko wo jɔjɔ su ni, na minɛ nbem afite tiɛntiɛn­bunɔɔ sɔɔ n kpiɛn n dika aba ase i ka, “U wa n wo má bɛrɛ. Nyini ti, má a dama ya Kere­fɔ n kun.”
translated: When Jesus saw the large crowd, he went up the mountain. He sat down, and his disciples came to him. He began to teach them, saying, “Blessed are the poor in spirit, for theirs is the kingdom of heaven.”

ref: As John's followers were going away, Jesus spoke to the crowds about John: What sort of person did you go out into the desert to see? Was he like tall grass blown about by the wind?

translated: When John heard in prison about the deeds of the Messiah, he sent his disciples to ask him, “Are you the one who is to come, or should we expect someone else? Are you the one who is to come, or should we look for another prophet who will come after you?”
